title: Raising the bar: Quality, shared responsibility, and the future of GitHub’s bug bounty program
content: GitHub’s bug‑bounty program is tightening its standards amid rising submission volumes. Only findings with a working proof of concept, clear impact, and validated scope will be accepted; generic or theoretical reports will be rejected. AI‑assisted research is welcomed as long as it is verified. GitHub clarifies that security incidents involving user‑trusted content fall under shared responsibility, not platform bypasses. Low‑risk, non‑exploitable reports will now receive swag instead of bounties to focus rewards on high‑impact discoveries.

title: 中国孩子正在经历肌肉力量危机 | 原文
content: China’s younger generations are experiencing a decline in muscle strength despite better nutrition, taller height, and more activity options. Over the past 20 years, kids’ performance in static long‑jump tests—a proxy for muscle health—has steadily worsened, especially in the last decade. Weak muscles can lead to lifelong reduced physical capacity, increased risk of chronic illness and falls, and lower independence in old age.

title: 被健康人群推崇的低碳水高蛋白，《Nature》子刊却说会早衰甚至早si？？？这是怎么回事？别的研究怎么说？ | 原文
content: Health communities praise low‑carb, high‑protein diets for weight loss and anti‑aging, but a study published in *npj Metabolic Health and Disease*, a Nature‑subsidiary journal, found that such a diet caused early ageing and disease in animals. The paper’s findings contrast with many clinical nutrition plans that advocate the same strategy, raising questions about the safety of low‑carb, high‑protein eating. The debate highlights a disconnect between popular dietary trends, clinical practice, and emerging research on metabolic health.

content: Termcat is a terminal that supports AIAgent command execution and includes two useful plugins. The “Claude Code Power” plugin manages multiple Claude startup configurations without altering system defaults and lets users view detailed Claude executions, such as file access and skill calls. The “AI Ops Agent” plugin assists with local and remote command execution and system maintenance, simplifying command recall and configuration. The project is hosted on GitHub and its homepage is https://termcat.uniline.site.

title: 好消息：微信有 AI 了，坏消息：是元宝...
content: WeChat now lets users forward chat logs to the Tencent AI app Yendou (Yuanbao) for summarization and task assistance. The feature is handy for work group minutes, travel plans, and quick reviews of long message threads, but its performance with files is unreliable and the workflow requires several taps. Tencent is investing heavily in AI and has announced plans for a WeChat‑native AI agent, yet the company remains cautious about integrating AI deeply into the platform. Yendou remains less popular than rivals like Doubao and Qianwen.

title: 如何看待 Anthropic 估值反超 OpenAI ？
content: Anthropic’s implied valuation reached $1 trillion on Forge Global, surpassing OpenAI’s $880 billion valuation on the same platform. Earlier in April, Anthropic’s annual recurring revenue (ARR) exceeded $30 billion, outpacing OpenAI’s disclosed $25 billion. This marks the first time Anthropic has led in both revenue and valuation since both companies were founded, signaling a shift in the competitive landscape within the AI industry.

title: 笑死，莫奈真迹被全网痛批是AI“废画”
content: A Twitter user uploaded a genuine Monet “Water Lilies” painting, labeling it “Made with AI” to provoke criticism. Internet users flooded the comments with detailed, often unfounded analyses, claiming stylistic flaws and lack of soul as if the work were machine‑generated. The experiment highlighted how the AI label triggers cognitive bias, leading people to dismiss quality art. It sparked discussion about the broader trend of “AI witch hunts” that erode trust and blindly devalue authentic human creations.

title: 月之暗面推出 Kimi WebBridge：让 AI 帮你操作浏览器
content: Moon Shadow has released Kimi WebBridge, a browser plugin that lets AI agents such as Kimi Code, Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Hermes Agent, and OpenClaw control the browser with the user’s login state, cookies, and account. It can click, scroll, type, fill forms, scrape data, and integrate cross‑site content, acting like the user while leaving the mouse and keyboard free. The tool automates repetitive web tasks, from data collection to complex financial research, and can be wrapped into a local CLI for repeatable workflows.

The AI industry saw several key moves on 15 May 2026: OpenAI launched Codex remote control in its ChatGPT app, Ant Group released the Ring‑2.6‑1T model, and Cline unveiled a modular SDK. The update enables real‑time code review and task execution from any device, with a secure relay that syncs session state across devices. The feature supports remote SSH, CI integration, and HIPAA‑compliant use in enterprise settings.

title: 2026 05 15 HackerNews
content: MIT faces funding and talent shortages; the president outlines new grant projects, industry partnerships, and policy advocacy. Anthropic releases Claude for Small Business, integrating with common tools and emphasizing manual review of generated content. Bun’s large codebase is rewritten in Rust, improving size and stability but still needs refinement. NVIDIA RTX 5090 eGPU setup on an M4 MacBook Air is technically possible but suffers from driver, virtualization, and performance limitations. Princeton eliminates its 133‑year honor‑code policy by requiring instructor proctoring to curb AI‑enabled cheating. “Scorched Earth 2000” launches a web version with multiplayer support, while patches can bypass restrictions. The $599 MacBook Neo, powered by the A18 Pro, offers good daily performance but 8 GB RAM limits multitasking. A Bitcoin wallet recovery and various enterprises use Claude for financial and technical insights. Cisco announces modest layoffs (<5 %) to focus on AI, offering training and placement. A new Zero‑day BitLocker exploit (YellowKey) allows unauthorized disk access via a USB stick. Multiple HackerNews threads discuss AI responsibilities, academic career challenges, and industry shifts, reflecting broader concerns about trust, labor, and technology governance.

title: Codex免费两个月，AI编程工具价格战开打，山姆·奥特曼亲自下场抢人
content: OpenAI announced that Codex will be free for two months to encourage company adoption, offering migration tools for a seamless switch. This follows Anthropic’s price hike for Claude Code, igniting a pricing war. Although Codex has advanced its coding, debugging, and agent capabilities, enterprise users still favor Claude due to established trust and lower perceived costs. Success for Codex hinges on maintaining performance and stability after the free trial, proving it can sustain enterprise workloads without escalating token expenses.

Musk appeared detached, reflecting a shift: Tesla’s dominance in China has eroded, with local brands like BYD, Xiaomi, and Huawei now leading the electric‑vehicle market. Lei Jun’s success illustrates how Xiaomi has adapted Tesla’s model to Chinese consumers, while Cook’s brief appearance signals Apple’s continued presence. Musk’s disengagement signals the end of his era of singular industry leadership.

title: A也通货膨胀了：ChatGPT让美国大学成绩单变成废纸 | 原文
content: A study of over 500,000 U.S. college transcripts found that after ChatGPT’s release, the share of A grades rose sharply in courses where AI can aid writing and programming. In these classes, A grades increased by about 30 %, while A‑ and B+ grades fell. The trend reflects a broader “grade inflation” that has accelerated with AI, echoing past increases at institutions such as Harvard.

title: 重构了自己 5 年前写的截图插件
content: A developer refactored a five‑year‑old Web screenshot plugin to enable editable canvas elements. The redesign splits the app into an application layer for workflow, a features layer for drawing tools, and a shared layer for reusable utilities. Global state is managed with MobX stores, separating crop box, toolbar, and drawing data. Each visual element is now an object stored in `canvasElements`, allowing selection, move, resize, and undo operations. Custom tools can register elements and their interaction logic, improving extensibility. The refactor also unifies screenshot source options and streamlines the build process.

title: 微软寻求收购 AI 公司以摆脱对 OpenAI 依赖
content: Microsoft is seeking to acquire an AI startup to reduce its dependence on OpenAI, secure talent, and meet its goal of developing advanced AI models by next year. After dropping an attempt to buy code‑generation firm Cursor due to regulatory concerns, Microsoft is negotiating with Inception, whose M12 venture fund invested $50 million in a 2025 seed round. The deal faces competition from SpaceX and other tech giants that are also interested in Inception.

title: Anthropic 高管：公司 90% 代码已由 AI 完成
content: Anthropic’s CFO reports that AI has written roughly 90 % of the company’s code, shifting white‑collar jobs from execution to oversight. The firm believes AI will not necessarily eliminate many positions but will automate most routine tasks, turning employees into supervisors of the AI systems.

title: Anthropic 企业采用率首超 OpenAI
content: Anthropic’s enterprise adoption rate rose 3.8 % to 34.4 % in April, surpassing OpenAI’s rate, which fell 2.9 % to 32.3 %. Over the past year, Anthropic’s adoption quadrupled, while OpenAI’s increased by only 0.3 %. The report cautions that Anthropic’s advantage may be fragile, citing rising costs, compute limits, and its token‑based billing model. The analysis tracked spending patterns of more than 50,000 U.S. businesses.

title: OpenAI 回应 TanStack 供应链攻击：未发现用户数据泄露
content: OpenAI released a statement regarding the “Mini Shai‑Hulud” supply‑chain attack on npm packages. Security analysts quickly investigated internal systems after detecting malicious activity targeting several popular packages and found no evidence of user data leaks or unauthorized access. While core services were not directly compromised, macOS users of OpenAI’s official applications must update their software by June 12, 2026.

title: Anthropic 推出 Claude for Small Business 瞄准中小企业 AI 应用洼地
content: Anthropic has released a suite called “Claude for Small Business,” targeting small and medium‑sized enterprises (SMEs). The package aims to lower AI adoption barriers for firms lacking IT resources by providing task‑automation tools, ready‑made workflows, and integrations with popular business software such as QuickBooks and PayPal. Anthropic also offers free AI training courses and plans in‑person workshops across U.S. cities, signaling a shift of AI platform focus from large enterprises to the SME market.

title: GitHub availability report: April 2026
content: In April 2026, GitHub experienced 10 service incidents, totaling about 90 hours of degraded performance across code search, audit logs, Copilot, GitHub Pages, Codespaces, code scanning, project boards, DNS, and search services. Causes included aggressive infrastructure upgrades, credential rotation failures, rate‑limit bugs, DNS misconfigurations, upstream outages, and scraping traffic overload. Incidents were resolved within hours, and the team implemented stronger testing, monitoring, gradual upgrades, and traffic controls to prevent recurrence.

content: Apple and OpenAI are reportedly deteriorating, with both companies expressing concern over the partnership’s future. The disagreement could lead to legal action over revenue sharing and contractual obligations. The dispute highlights the growing tensions between major tech firms on AI collaboration, potentially affecting market dynamics and regulatory scrutiny.

title: From latency to instant: Modernizing GitHub Issues navigation performance
content: GitHub Issues reduced perceived latency by shifting data flow to the client. A client‑side IndexedDB cache with stale‑while‑revalidate lets soft React navigations render instantly, while a preheating strategy populates the cache for likely next issues. A service worker intercepts hard and Turbo navigations, serving thin shells and letting React hydrate from local data. These changes increased instant navigations from 4 % to ~30 %, lowered median load times below one second, and improved lower‑percentile performance, though hard‑start paths still lag.
